sanskrit:
वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि।
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा-
न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही॥
transliteration: vāsāṁsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya navāni gṛhṇāti naro ’parāṇi | tathā śarīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇāny anyāni saṁyāti navāni dehī ||
literal_meaning: As a person casts off worn-out garments and takes others that are new, so the embodied soul casts off worn-out bodies and enters others that are new.
contextual_meaning: The self is not the perishable body but the eternal witness; clinging to bodily identity fuels grief, whereas dis-identifying allows calm acceptance of necessary endings.
psychological_state: Arjuna experiences acute death-anxiety and survivor-guilt; Krishna offers a cognitive reframe that loosens over-identification with the body, initiating post-traumatic growth.
trigger_scenario: A paramedic freezes at an accident scene, overwhelmed by the sight of a dying child, and later avoids ambulances due to intrusive memories—until therapy reframes death as transition rather than failure.
anchor_text: You are not your body; let it change like clothes.
